name: Chow Mein with Shrimp and Pork
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Cornstarch, dissolved in 2 teaspoons Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Drop noodles into boiling water and boil for five minutes. Rinse under cold water. Drain.
2. Mix with 1 tablespoon oil. Set aside.
3. Mix shrimp with 1/4 teaspoon salt and cornstarch.
4. Heat 1 tablespoon oil to 400°F in a wok and stir fry shrimp until they turn pink, about 1 minute. Remove.
5. Heat 1 tablespoon oil to 400°F. Stir fry pork until color changes. Add sherry, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, and sugar. Stir fry 1 minute. Remove.
6. Heat 1 tablespoon oil to 400°F. Stir fry bok choy for 1 minute. Add pork, mushrooms, 1 teaspoon salt, and 2 tablespoons soy sauce. Add stock. Cook for 3 minutes.
7. Thicken with dissolved cornstarch. Pour in shrimp and bring to boil. Remove.
8. Heat 3 tablespoons oil to 400°F. Turn down to 350°F and stir fry noodles until the outside is golden brown but the inside is soft (They will form a cake). Cook for 5 minutes on each side. Remove to platter.
9. Place cooked mixture on top of noodles.
